title: Living in Boulder vlog 4/24/18: Google Earthing Boulder

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs at 2:56 AM about resuming a long-dormant Google Earth 3D reconstruction project of Boulder, Colorado. He explains he's uploading historical photos (from the 1850s–1970s) to Flickr and linking them to a KML file, and plans to build a polygon-based 3D model of Boulder with two versions — a lot-color version and a realistic version. He also briefly mentions burning out on a Spanish audio history book project he was working on before.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Avoiding it — too expensive at $700 and fears getting carried away and never finishing
Excited to potentially finish it after a year-and-a-half hiatus
Burned out on it but made progress adding links

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's 256 AM on April 25th.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a video for a few days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't know how many days I've been really high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My shoulder has been kind of sore.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last time I did a video, I was working on my free audio history book page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The audio history book has three different levels of Spanish and is designed to read.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I burned out on working on the audio history book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I added links to the audio history book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to look at a really interesting project that I started a long time ago and never finished.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The project is Google Earth Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started working on Google Earth Boulder today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I noticed on my Google Earth Boulder that I made before, I had advantage points from all the different photos of Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found out where the photos were taken and what angle they were taken from.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I put markers and lines in the direction of the photos on Google Earth Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a bunch of photos that I never uploaded to Flickr.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to upload the photos individually to Flickr because I put them in each photo's own Boulder folder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't remember why I put each photo in its own folder, but I must have had a good reason.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to wait to upload the photos because it will take a lot of time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't decide if I want to put all the photos in one folder or organize them by decades or years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Flickr now allows infinite folders within folders.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have about a thousand photos to upload.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never uploaded the folders I collected from the Boulder Public Library.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Many of the photos from the Boulder Public Library are watermarked.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to attach the photos to my KMZ file so that when you click on it, it shows the picture.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm halfway through the 1890s photos right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started with photos from the 1850s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The 1870s, 1890s, and 1990s have the most photos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are hundreds of photos just from the 1890s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There was a photographer named Mr. Photographer who lived in Boulder and took many photos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There was a bike club in Boulder, and there's a photo of them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited to finish Google Earth Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to have two versions of Google Earth Boulder: one showing all the lots with different colors and one realistic version.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm using the polygon tool instead of Sketchup because Sketchup costs seven hundred dollars.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might get carried away with Sketchup and never finish the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to rebuild Boulder and put all the photos into YouTube videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll work on the project as long as I can, even if I burn out after two weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder Canyon and Central City were just as developed as Europe in the 1870s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There were trains all over the mountains and towns in the 1870s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's going to be fun to build the historical maps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's three in the morning, and I'm going to go back to working on the project.
